summary: Ukrainian air defenses shot down 79 hostile UAVs and intercepted an Iskander-M ballistic missile during a large-scale attack on June 22. Russian forces raised their flag on Preobrazhenskaya Street in Konstantinovka, indicating continued advances in the south. Two Tu-160 bombers were relocated from the Far East to Olenya airfield, reflecting Russian strategic redeployment.
key_points: Ukrainian forces downed 79 enemy UAVs and repelled an Iskander-M missile attack across multiple regions (Source: @kpszsu/66001).
Russian assault units of the Southern Group of Forces raised the Russian flag on Preobrazhenskaya Street in Konstantinovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31078).
Two Tu-160 strategic bombers were transferred from the Far East to Olenya airfield (Source: @eRadarrua/97710).
Multiple reactive UAVs were reported over Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Sumy, and Dnipropetrovsk regions, heading toward various localities (Source: @kpszsu/66002, @kpszsu/66006, @kpszsu/65981, @kpszsu/66001).
Ukrainian security services detained two FSB agents planning a bomb attack in Kyiv's Shevchenkivskyi district (Source: @UkraineNow/82110).
Power outages were reported in occupied Crimea due to drone attacks on critical infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82097).
Russian drones struck the FSB building in Armynske and caused explosions in multiple Crimean locations (Source: @UkraineNow/82107).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian air defenses downed 79 hostile UAVs and intercepted an Iskander-M missile during a coordinated strike on June 22, demonstrating robust anti‑air capabilities. Russian forces continued their ground push, raising the national flag on Preobrazhenskaya Street in Konstantinovka, signaling ongoing offensive operations in the south. Strategic Russian assets were shifted as two Tu‑160 bombers moved to Olenya airfield, indicating adjustments in long‑range strike posture.

points: Ukrainian air defense units reported shooting down 79 Shahed, Gerbera, and Italmas UAVs across the north, south, and east, and intercepting an Iskander‑M ballistic missile launched from Crimea (Source: @kpszsu/66001).
Security services arrested two FSB agents in Kyiv who were preparing a remote‑detonated bomb attack on a municipal building in Shevchenkivskyi district (Source: @UkraineNow/82110).

Ukrainian forces reported multiple reactive UAV sightings: over Chernihiv (Novhorod‑Siverskyi area), Kharkiv (Bohoduhiv, Chuhuiv), Sumy (heading to Shostka), and Dnipropetrovsk (heading to Pavlohrad) (Source: @kpszsu/66002, @kpszsu/66006, @kpszsu/65981).
In the Dnipropetrovsk region, over 30 attacks injured five people, including a 2‑year‑old child, and damaged agricultural fields and private homes (Source: @UkraineNow/82101).
Ukrainian intelligence noted that Russian drone relay stations in Belarus remain a target, with Zelensky threatening unilateral removal (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31063).
Russian Southern Group of Forces raised the Russian Federation flag on liberated Preobrazhenskaya Street in Konstantinovka, continuing urban advances (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31078).
Two Tu‑160 strategic bombers were relocated from the Far East to the Olenya airfield, indicating a shift in long‑range strike deployment (Source: @eRadarrua/97710).

Russian drones attacked the FSB building in the occupied settlement of Armynske, causing detonations, and also struck locations in Feodosia, Sevastopol, and the Tavriyskaya power plant in Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/82107).
In occupied Crimea, power outages affected the north‑west, central, and south‑coastal energy zones after drone attacks on critical infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82097).
Russian forces reported continued offensive operations in the southwestern part of Konstantinovka, targeting enemy groups (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31078).
Reactive UAVs were observed over Chernihiv region near Novhorod‑Siverskyi, indicating potential reconnaissance or strike missions (Source: @eRadarrua/97737).
Multiple UAVs entered Sumy from the north, heading toward Shostka and other localities, suggesting pressure on the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/66002, @kpszsu/65957).
UAV activity reported from the Black Sea toward Serhiyivka and Zatoka, highlighting maritime‑to‑land threat vectors (Source: @kpszsu/66006).
A group of UAVs from Kursk region moved northward into Sumy, targeting Shostka (Source: @kpszsu/65957).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV over the Beresny area in Chernihiv, continuing surveillance in the north‑eastern sector (Source: @eRadarrua/97706).
UAVs were detected over Sumy from the north, heading toward Svesa and Yampil, indicating pressure on the northern border (Source: @kpszsu/66000).
UAVs from the Black Sea were observed heading toward Serhiyivka and Zatoka, suggesting a southern approach to the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/66006).
Reactive UAVs reported in Chernihiv region near Novhorod‑Siverskyi (Source: @eRadarrua/97737).
UAVs from the north were seen over Kharkiv, Bohoduhiv, and Chuhuiv, moving toward Pavlohrad, showing cross‑regional activity (Source: @kpszsu/66002).
A group of UAVs from Kursk moved north into Sumy, targeting Shostka (Source: @kpszsu/65957).
Russian forces raised their flag in Konstantinovka, indicating ongoing advances in the southern sector (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31078).
UAV attacks on the FSB building in Armynske and explosions in Feodosia and Sevastopol reflect heightened drone activity in the south (Source: @UkraineNow/82107).
Power outages in occupied Crimea were linked to drone strikes on critical infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82097).
UAVs from the Black Sea headed toward Serhiyivka and Zatoka, targeting southern coastal areas (Source: @kpszsu/66006).
UAVs reported over Zaporizhzhia from the south, indicating pressure on the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/66000).
Ukrainian defenses downed 79 hostile UAVs and intercepted an Iskander‑M missile launched from Crimea, with engagements across multiple regions (Source: @kpszsu/66001).
Russian drones struck the FSB building in Armynske and caused detonations in several Crimean locations, including Feodosia and Sevastopol (Source: @UkraineNow/82107).
Ukrainian air defenses reported destroying 168 enemy UAVs over Russian territory on a separate day, showing extended reach (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31068).
A drone attack on the Dubna Space Communications Center in Moscow caused no casualties but was noted by TASS (Source: @tass_agency/381885).

Ukrainian forces reported multiple reactive UAV sightings across Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Sumy, and Dnipropetrovsk, indicating widespread aerial activity (Source: @kpszsu/66002, @kpszsu/66006, @kpszsu/65981).

key_developments: Ukrainian air defenses downed 79 UAVs and intercepted an Iskander‑M missile across multiple regions, demonstrating strong anti‑air capability (Source: @kpszsu/66001).
Russian Southern Group of Forces raised the Russian flag on Preobrazhenskaya Street in Konstantinovka, marking continued ground advances (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31078).
Two Tu‑160 strategic bombers were relocated from the Far East to Olenya airfield, indicating Russian strategic redeployment (Source: @eRadarrua/97710).
FSB agents were arrested in Kyiv for planning a remote‑detonated bomb attack on a municipal building (Source: @UkraineNow/82110).
Ukrainian forces reported extensive reactive UAV activity over Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Sumy, and Dnipropetrovsk, targeting multiple localities (Source: @kpszsu/66002, @kpszsu/66006).
Drone attacks caused power outages in occupied Crimea, affecting north‑west, central, and south‑coastal energy zones (Source: @UkraineNow/82097).
Russian drones struck the FSB building in Armynske and caused explosions in Feodosia, Sevastopol, and the Tavriyskaya power plant (Source: @UkraineNow/82107).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian air defenses have proven effective against large UAV swarms and ballistic missile threats, reducing immediate aerial danger. Russian ground forces continue incremental advances in the south, as evidenced by flag‑raising in Konstantinovka, while strategic bomber redeployment suggests a focus on long‑range strike capability. Persistent drone activity across multiple fronts indicates both sides are leveraging UAVs for reconnaissance and strike, maintaining a high‑tempo conflict environment.
sections.regional_response: Western intelligence sources note increased Ukrainian resilience, highlighted by successful interdiction of UAV attacks and arrests of hostile agents. Russian authorities are reinforcing southern offensives and adjusting strategic assets, such as relocating Tu‑160 bombers, to sustain pressure. Neighboring states monitor the escalation, particularly concerning cross‑border UAV incursions and potential spillover effects.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV flight paths is essential to anticipate target selection and allocate air defense resources. The relocation of strategic bombers may precede changes in long‑range strike patterns, requiring updated threat modeling. Ground advances in the south should be tracked for potential encirclement of key logistics hubs, while the arrest of FSB operatives underscores ongoing covert operations within Kyiv.
